characters.name: Alan Bruce
characters.description: Alan Bruce isJean Wilson's partner played by Trevor Adams. The Wedding Party

characters.name: Alice
characters.description: AliceisRoger's wife played byUna Stubbs(Sherlock). She and Roger were the first friends to arrive. The Anniversary

characters.name: Alice Richards
characters.description: Alice Richards was a character in the first episode of series 2:Communication Problems. She was notable for having poor hearing. Mrs Richards was unsatisfied with her stay at the hotel and complained toBasil Fawlty, who was very unsympathetic. Later she claimed that some money had been stolen from her room, when in fact she had left it behind accidentally at a shop. This ended up with Basil losing the money he had won on a horse and smashing a vase belonging to her. She is last seen demanding compensation from the Fawltys as Sybil Fawlty counts out the cost of the smashed vase from Basil's winnings.

characters.name: Andre
characters.description: Andre is a local restauranteur played by André Maranne. Gourmet Night Andre visited Fawlty Towers one Sunday night to discuss plans for Gourmet Night. On the night when Kurt was drunk, Basil asked Andre to do the cooking (duck) with orange and cherry sauces.

characters.name: Anne Bryce
characters.description: Mrs. Anne Bryce is a friend ofRachel Lloyd. She was a possible wedding guest. The Wedding Party

characters.name: Arthur
characters.description: ArthurisVirginia's husband played byRobert Arnold. He and Virginia brought cake for the Fawlty's fifteenth anniversary. The Anniversary

characters.name: Audrey
characters.description: Audrey is Sybils best friend played by Christine Shaw. The Wedding Party (mentioned) The Anniversary

characters.name: Basil D'Oliveira
characters.description: Basil D'Oliveira was a England international cricketeer. A Touch of Class(mentioned in newspaper)

characters.name: Basil Fawlty
characters.description: Basil Fawlty  Basil Fawlty, played byJohn Cleese, is a snobbish and miserly misanthrope who is desperate to belong to a higher social class. He sees a successful hotel as a means of achieving this ("turn it into an establishment of class...") yet his job forces him to be pleasant to people he despises. He is known for his caustic tongue and wit, muttering angry little asides under his breath when feeling taxed. He wants his hotel to be exemplary, but he is cheap, incompetent, arrogant, short-tempered and often looking to take shortcuts. His wife,Sybil Fawlty, seems more equipped to run the place, but she is often absent.

characters.name: Basil Fawlty
characters.description: Basil Fawlty  Basil Fawlty, played byJohn Cleese, is a snobbish and miserly misanthrope who is desperate to belong to a higher social class. He sees a successful hotel as a means of achieving this ("turn it into an establishment of class...") yet his job forces him to be pleasant to people he despises. He is known for his caustic tongue and wit, muttering angry little asides under his breath when feeling taxed. He wants his hotel to be exemplary, but he is cheap, incompetent, arrogant, short-tempered and often looking to take shortcuts. His wife,Sybil Fawlty, seems more equipped to run the place, but she is often absent.

characters.name: Bennion
characters.description: Bennion is a deliveryman played by George Lee. He was delivering a garden gnome to Fawlty Towers.Manuelthought he wanted room 16. The Builders

characters.name: Bill Morton
characters.description: Bill Morton is the Fawlty's mutual friend. He was named after the shows vision mixer. The Hotel Inspectors(mentioned)

characters.name: Brenda
characters.description: Brendais a friend ofSybil Fawlty. She is possibly one of her golfing girlfriends. A Touch of Class

characters.name: Brian
characters.description: Brian is hotel inspector number two played by Peter Brett. "Why don't you have dinner here and Chris and I can try the Claremont?" The Hotel Inspectors

characters.name: Chris
characters.description: Chris is hotel inspector number three played by Andy Williams. The Hotel Inspectors

characters.name: Colonel & Mrs Hall
characters.description: Colonel and Mrs Petal Hall are JPs played by Allan Cuthbertson and Ann Way. Gourmet Night Colonel and Mrs Hall arrived just after the Coosters rang to cancel. Basil apparently met Colonel Hall at a golf dinner the previous year. Basil was so nervous at Colonel Halls twitch, he forgot his own name. He and Sybil accidentally called Mrs Hall Mrs Small. Mrs Hall was later served a raw mullet.

characters.name: Constable Graham
characters.description: Constable Graham is police constable number one. He askedBasil Fawltyto escort him andConstable Nudgeto room twenty three. There had been reports that screaming was coming from inside.

characters.name: Constable Nudge
characters.description: Constable Ericson Nudge is police constable number two. He is also referred to as Officer Ericson. The Robbers

characters.name: Danny Brown
characters.description: Danny Brown is a CID (Criminal Investigation Department) agent played by Robin Ellis. A Touch of Class Danny stayed in room 7 for a while and was admiring one of Polly's sketches at lunchtime.

characters.name: Doris Hare
characters.description: Doris Hare is a middle aged spinster played by Dorothy Frere. She was staying with her friendMiss Gurke. Waldorf Salad

characters.name: Doug and Judy Norman
characters.description: Doug and Judy Norman are Floridian tourists. They were visiting Torquay for their first anniversary. The Robbers

characters.name: Dr Finn
characters.description: Dr Finn is a doctor played by Louis Mahoney. He works atNorthwick Park Hospital. The Germans

characters.name: Dr Price
characters.description: Dr Price is a doctor played by Geoffrey Palmer. He presumably works atNorthwick Park Hospital. The Kipper and the Corpse The Robbers(mentioned)

characters.name: Electrician
characters.description: The Electrician was mentioned during one ofSybil Fawlty's telephone conversations withAudrey. The Hotel Inspectors

characters.name: Elsie
characters.description: Elsie is Fawlty Towers predecessor. She used to help Polly with housekeeping. She later emigrated to Canada. The Germans(mentioned)

characters.name: Erica Praline
characters.description: Erica Praline is a young tourist who finds Basil charming. She was in room twenty six. The Robbers

characters.name: Ferdinand
characters.description: Ferdinand is a waiter who works at Andre's restaurant. Gourmet Night (uncredited) Ferdinand took the duck away while Basil was talking to Andre. Basil then collected the wrong plate.

characters.name: Flynn Eiderdown
characters.description: Flynn Eiderdown is the manager ofThe E'chouer Suites. He was shot byThe Terrors of Torquayand was hospitalised. The Robbers(mentioned)

characters.name: George
characters.description: George is a friend of the Fawlty's and Audrey's partner. The Wedding Party

characters.name: Gill Terence
characters.description: Gill Terence is an ambulance driver. "I'm afraid that's unlikely, Mr Malty, seeing that you're not there." The Robbers

characters.name: Harold Robbins
characters.description: Harold Robbins was a famous author. Some of his books includeThe Carpetbaggers,Never Love a Strangerand79 Park Avenue. His real name is Francis Kane.

characters.name: Harry Hamilton
characters.description: Harry Hamilton is a demanding and irascible American played by Bruce Boa. As he was in no mood for games, he paid Basil twenty pounds to keepTerryon for longer. He and Mrs Hamilton left after Basil let the steaks burn. Waldorf Salad

characters.name: Horses
characters.description: Horses were mentioned in Communication Problems. Basil won money on a horse named Dragonfly. Other horses include: Birdbrain

characters.name: Inglebert and Ethelinda
characters.description: Inglebert and Ethelinda are elderly Germans guests who wanted to hire a car. Basil misunderstood them and thought they were volunteering to go out and buy some meat. The Germans (uncredited)

characters.name: Irene Young
characters.description: Miss Irene Young is a business woman played by Pamela Buchner. She was one of Mr Leeman's cordial colleagues. The Kipper and the Corpse

characters.name: Jantzen and Kirstie Vandersloop
characters.description: Jantzen and Kirstie Vandersloop are a Scandinavian couple. They were in room fourteen. The Robbers

characters.name: Jean Wilson
characters.description: Jean Wilson isPolly Sherman's schoolmate played by April Walker. She is alsoPhillip Lloyd's stepdaughter. The Wedding Party

characters.name: Johannes Brahms
characters.description: Johannes Brahms was a German composer and pianist. Basil Fawltywas listening to his music in the office. Basil was most insulted whenSybil Fawltycalled it racket.

characters.name: John
characters.description: John is hotel inspector #1 played by Geoffrey Morris. The Hotel Inspectors "Twenty six bedrooms. Twelve with private bathrooms."

characters.name: Kaufmann Kerr
characters.description: Kaufmann Kerr is a guest played by George Lee. Communication Problems

characters.name: Kingswood Higgins
characters.description: Kingswood Higgins is a guest played by Stuart Sherwin. Basil the Rat

characters.name: Kitty
characters.description: KittyisReg's wife played by Denyse Alexander. She is possibly one of Sybil's golfing girlfriends. The Anniversary

characters.name: Kurt
characters.description: Kurtis the Greek Continental chef atFawlty Towers. He works at the hotel on gourmet nights, for fifty pounds a night. He was recommended toMr. Fawltyby his favourite restauranteur,Andre, who trains him in the culinary art. He is homosexual and an alcoholic. He has a crush on Manuel, but Manuel does not return Kurt’s feelings. Consequently, he drinks himself into unconsciousness; causing him unable to cook for the gourmet night at Fawlty Towers and leading to Basil ruining said gourmet night. After this event, Basil never hires him ever again.

characters.name: Laundrymen
characters.description: The Laundrymen appear briefly at the end of The Kipper and the Corpse

characters.name: Lionel Twitchen
characters.description: Lionel Twitchen (pronounced Twychen) is one of Torquay's leading Rotarians played by Richard Caldicot. Gourmet Night Mr and Mrs Twitchen drank tomato juice while chatting with the Halls. Mr Twitchen was also Treasurer of 1975.

characters.name: Lord Melbury
characters.description: Lord Melburyis a con-man who passed himself off as an aristocrat. He was played byMichael Gwynn. Melbury showed up to the hotel looking for a room for a few nights, to whichBasilresponded with annoyance since he hadn't booked ahead. As Melbury fills out the forms, Basil takes a call fromMr. O'Reilly, but when Melbury doesn't put down his first name Basil gets more impatient. Melbury then reveals that he is infact Lord Melbury and Basil, eager to attract to a "higher class" of customer, decides to start kissing up to him in order to please him. Later at lunch he moves other guests to make room for Melbury at a table by the window, annoying them. When Melbury actually comes in and goes to sit down Basil pulls the seat out, unintentionally causing him to fall to the ground and knocking the table over.

characters.name: Lurphy, Kerr and Jones
characters.description: Lurphy, Kerr and Jones are incompetent Irish builders. Lurphy- Micahel Cronin Kerr- Barney Dorman

characters.name: Major Gowen
characters.description: Major Gowen Major Gowen, played by Ballard Berkeley, is a slightly senile, amiable retired old soldier who is a permanent resident atthe hotel. He is one of the few guests whomBasilseems to like. This is because of his former military status, making him a symbol of the establishment status that Basil craves. He is often introduced as their "oldest resident", howeverMiss Tibbsat one point refers to herself andMiss Gatsbyas the "oldest residents". He enjoys talking about the world outside, especially the cricket scores and workers' strikes, and is always on the lookout for the newspaper.
